name: Dark Pool Flow
description: Exploit institutional dark pool / OTC positioning signals that haven't yet moved the lit market. Buy ATM/OTM options on mid/large-cap stocks where sustained directional dark pool flow confirms accumulation or distribution. News catalysts (buybacks, M&A, earnings) provide context for interpreting flow patterns.
edge: Institutional flow detection — passive buying/selling in dark pools precedes lit price moves by days to weeks. Material catalysts like buybacks explain structural buying that might otherwise appear as noise.
instruments: ATM/OTM calls or puts, 2-6 week expiry. Bull/bear vertical spreads for defined risk.

command_details.scan: Quick dark pool ranking — fetches 5-day dark pool data only, skips options flow for speed. During market hours, data is automatically interpolated (partial today blended with prior days). Use to identify candidates.
command_details.evaluate: Full 7-milestone analysis — dark pool + options flow + OI changes + news/catalysts + analyst ratings. During market hours, dark pool data is automatically interpolated. Detects conflicts between dark pool and options flow. Use for trade decisions.
command_details.discover: Market-wide candidate discovery — scans flow alerts across all tickers, excludes watchlist/portfolio. Dark pool data interpolated during market hours.

name: LEAP IV Mispricing
description: Buy long-dated options where implied volatility is significantly below realized volatility. Profits from IV expansion as the market reprices the option toward fair vol.
edge: Single-asset vol mispricing — HV20/HV60 exceeds LEAP IV by ≥15 points, IV rank below 50th percentile.
instruments: LEAP calls (6-18 month expiry), 30-50Δ. Defined risk = premium paid.

name: GARCH Convergence Spreads
description: Exploit differential GARCH repricing speed across correlated assets during vol regime shifts. When a catalyst elevates realized vol in a sector, components reprice IV at different rates — buy the lagger's underpriced options.
edge: Cross-asset vega arbitrage — GARCH lag between correlated pairs creates measurable IV divergence (>1σ from 60-day mean).
instruments: 30-40Δ calls on the lagger, 3-6 month expiry. Optional short vega on the leader.

name: Risk Reversal
description: Exploit IV skew by selling OTM puts and buying OTM calls (bullish) or vice versa (bearish). Costless or credit-generating directional exposure that profits from the structural put/call IV imbalance.
edge: IV skew — OTM puts trade at significantly higher IV than equivalent-delta OTM calls. Selling the rich side funds the cheap side.
instruments: Sell OTM put (25-50Δ) + Buy OTM call (25-50Δ), 2-8 week expiry.

name: Volatility-Credit Gap v2 (VCG-R)
description: Detects when VIX is elevated (>28) but credit (HYG) hasn't repriced — a dangerous divergence that resolves violently. VIX gate INVERTED from v1 (was <40, now >28). VVIX is a severity amplifier (Tier 1/2/3), no longer a hard gate. HDR and credit 5d gate removed. Fires rarely (~0.26/year) with high conviction. Counter-signal BOUNCE fires when VCG < -3.5 (credit overshot).
edge: VIX/VVIX vs HYG regression residual identifies unresolved vol-credit divergence. RO: VIX>28 + VCG>2.5σ. EDR watch: VIX>25 + VCG>2.0σ. Event capture rate: 71% vs 29% for v1 (2018-2025 backtest).
instruments: HYG puts (ATM/OTM, 1-3 week expiry). Bear put spreads on HYG/JNK for defined risk. Tier 1 = max hedging, Tier 2 = standard, EDR = half-Kelly.
target_rr: Portfolio hedge — sized relative to credit exposure

name: Crash Risk Index (CRI)
description: Detect CTA deleveraging cascades by monitoring VIX/VVIX rising, COR1M implied correlation spiking, and SPX breaking below 100-day MA. Systematic CTA funds (~$400B AUM) use vol-targeting — when realized vol doubles, they halve equity exposure mechanically over 3-5 days.
edge: CTA mechanical selling is predictable and time-bound. Convergence of VIX, VVIX, COR1M implied correlation, and momentum signals precedes forced deleveraging cascades.
instruments: SPY puts (ATM/OTM, 2-4 week expiry). Bear put spreads on SPY for defined risk. Tail hedges.
target_rr: Portfolio hedge — sized relative to equity exposure
